stemHtml: Some large aeroplanes make use of main-wheel steering as well as their nose-wheel steering...

options.a: to assist the pilot to automatically control the straight ground roll path during takeoffs and landings.
options.b: during low speed taxiing and manoeuvring only to improve manoeuvrability and reduce excessive abrasion of the main wheel tyres.
options.c: during low- and high-speed taxi operations to improve the steering characteristics and stability of the aircraft.
options.d: for manoeuvring the aircraft during towing operations only, and it is deactivated when the aircraft moves under its own power.
